hypothesis: Pretraining X-MoPA via cross-modal latent prediction will produce joint embeddings such that, when one modality (text, graph, or XRD) is missing during finetuning, downstream property predictors will retain at least 90% of their full-modal MAE performance (i.e., MAE increase ≤10%), whereas equivalent models pretrained with simple concatenation or contrastive-only objectives will exhibit >25% MAE degradation under the same missing-modality condition.
